tweet: sdk got an upgrade today. speech-to-text and media uploads are now built directly into the npm package. also taught the media service how to name files correctly on download, which is nice. step-3.5-flash is back in the routing pool too.
alt_tweet: turns out people prefer when downloaded files have actual names instead of cryptic hashes. media service now sets Content-Disposition properly. also wired up stt and media uploads in the sdk, and fixed step-3.5-flash routing.
image_prompt: Cozy 8-bit pixel art. Polly the cute bee mascot (yellow body, brown stripes, tiny blue wings, wearing a tiny toolbelt) high-fiving a chunky pixel robot with a CRT monitor head. The robot is holding a neatly labeled digital file folder, while the round tan nomnom creature happily eats a pile of messy, glowing red hash strings in the background. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. High contrast, dynamic and energetic composition.
reasoning: Leads directly with the SDK upgrade. Uses dry humor regarding the file naming fix (a classic small but annoying dev problem). Keeps it brief and lowercase.
